senses.definition: the process or trade of manufacturing slate products or working with slate stone.
examples: He studied slatemaking as part of his apprenticeship.
The quarry supplied material for slatemaking in the small town.

senses.definition: a person who works with slate, especially one who splits slate into thinner pieces for roofing or flooring.
examples: The slater laid new slate tiles on the roof.
In the old town, a skilled slater was respected for his precise cuts.

senses.definition: plural of slater; multiple people who work with slate.
examples: The village relied on the slaters for roofing work.
During the festival, several slaters demonstrated slate-shaping techniques.

senses.definition: thin pieces of slate used for roofing tiles or writing slates; or schedules of proposed items.
examples: The roof was covered with gray slates.
The teacher wiped the slate clean after taking notes.
The committee added several items to the slate of topics.

senses.definition: a list of candidates or items to be considered; a schedule of possible actions.
examples: The party announced a slate of candidates for the election.
We reviewed the slate of proposed regulations.

senses.definition: a place or business where slate is cut or prepared for use; slate production facility.
examples: The slateworks shut down after the market declined.
Slateworks produced durable roofing slabs.

senses.definition: like slate in color or appearance; slate-colored; dull gray.
examples: The dog had a slatey coat that blended with the roof.
The slatey skies warned of rain.

senses.definition: a yard or place where slate is stored or processed.
examples: Stacks of slate awaited transport from the slateyard.
The slateyard smelled of minerals and dust.

senses.definition: an area of ground or a narrow passage; archaic or dialectal term; also used as a scratch or scrape; may appear in historical texts.
examples: We found a slath of ancient slate in the mine.
The old map shows a slath near the quarry.

senses.definition: to spread a thick layer of something (often a substance like butter, cream, or paint) on a surface.
examples: She slathered butter on the warm bread.
The painter slathered blue paint across the wall.

senses.definition: a thick layer of something spread across a surface.
examples: A generous slather of sunscreen protected her skin.
The pancakes had a thick slather of syrup.

senses.definition: covered with a thick layer of something spread on a surface.
examples: Her face was slathered with sunscreen.
The bread was slathered with butter.

senses.definition: spread a thick layer of something on a surface.
examples: He had slathered on lotion before going outside.
The toast was slathered with jam.

senses.definition: present participle of slather; spreading thickly.
examples: She was slathering sunscreen on before the hike.
He spent the morning slathering paint onto the fence.

senses.definition: the act of spreading a thick layer of something.
examples: A little slathering goes a long way with cream.
The chef noted the slathering of butter on the bread.

senses.definition: third person singular present of slather; spreads thickly.
examples: She slathers sunscreen on every morning.
The chef slathers butter on the toast.

senses.definition: more slatelike in appearance or more slender and flat like slats.
examples: The slatier design looked lighter on the eye.
They preferred a slatier, more minimalist aesthetic.

senses.definition: most slat-like; having the qualities of slats to the greatest degree.
examples: The slatiest room was hung with wooden slats along the walls.
Their slatiest design featured many horizontal lines.

senses.definition: decorated with or divided into slats; arranged in slats.
examples: The ceiling was slatified with thin wooden panels.
A slatified screen provided privacy.

senses.definition: to convert or arrange into slats; to subdivide with slats.
examples: They decided to slatify the door for ventilation.
The screen was slatified to allow light through.

senses.definition: present participle of slatify; making into slats or slat-like.
examples: They were slatifying panels for the new fence.
The process involved slatifying the boards.

senses.definition: the quality of being like or consisting of slats; slender and flat.
examples: The slatiness of the room created a light, airy feel.
Architects noted the slatiness of the façade.

senses.definition: plural of slatiness.
examples: The slatinesses of the screens varied in width.
We compared the slatinesses of different wooden panels.

senses.definition: present participle of slate; installing slate tiles or setting a schedule for criticism or censure (slating someone).
examples: The workers were slating the roof with slate tiles.
They faced criticism, but the board began slating topics for discussion.

senses.definition: plural of slating; instances of slating tiles or scheduling items.
examples: The slatings of roof repairs were completed in a week.
The commission's slatings covered several topics.

senses.definition: resembling or characteristic of a slat; having slat-like appearance.
examples: The slatish panels gave the room a modern look.
A slatish texture stained the wall.

senses.definition: thin flat pieces of wood or other material that form a part of a shutter, blind, or fencing.
examples: Venetian blinds are made of slats.
The fence was built from evenly spaced slats.

senses.definition: made of or divided into slats; arranged with slats.
examples: A slatted chair allowed air to circulate.
The ceiling was slatted with wooden strips.

senses.definition: someone who slates; a person who criticizes or scolds harshly (informal).
examples: The boss was known as a harsh slatter.
No one wanted to work with the notorious slatter.
